title: Tanker Shipping - May 19, 2026
summary: The VLCC tanker market is experiencing increased uncertainty due to geopolitical risks and regional access constraints, particularly in the Arabian Gulf and Atlantic basin, leading to a widening gap in freight rates. Global oil prices are expected to rise above $90 per barrel due to structural deficits from the Strait of Hormuz disruption and growing inventory depletion, while a robust orderbook suggests potential downward pressure on rates longer term.
